observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to – 
(a)	cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU officials/employees; and 

(b)	ensure that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor for review. The necessary documents must be attached therein, such as the official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, etc. Henceforth, introduce controls that would assure that cash advances are liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the guidelines on the proper accounting and reporting of the receipt and utilization of cash donations in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and personnel concerned to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the claims.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Head of the
Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee and the Municipal Accountant to pursue
the completion of the physical count of all PPEs owned by the Municipality as well as the
reconciliation of both their records to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate
and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Disposal Committee to
prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs to avoid further deterioration and
possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the adjusting entries to correct the RPT/SET Receivables as of December
31, 2021 and henceforth record the current year’s receivables for RPT/SET
receivables every beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies and materials for stock
under the Inventory account and the issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take
place under the appropriate expense account;
b. Inventory Committee Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit monthly
SSMI based on the RIS to the Municipal Accounting Office in support to the JEV
recognizing the issued materials as expenses. Also, the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team the SSMI for issued materials for verification;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each
commodity/stock to facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the year-end
balances; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct of physical count of all supplies and materials
inventory of the Municipality every semester and to prepare and render a complete
Report on Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the COA Audit Team not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second semesters,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to enforce the liquidation of cash
advances in accordance with its prescribed period and comply strictly in the
granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular
No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer
to remit the balance of P1,000,336.46 to the concerned agency pursuant COA
Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used to finance other projects or by other
agencies needing assistance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain stock cards and/or records of the detailed
composition of the PPE accounts, identify the recorded accumulated
depreciation for each type of PPE, allocate depreciation separately on
each items or part of the PPE accounts and effect the necessary
corrections/adjustment thereto.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Identify the semi-expendable properties included in the PPE accounts,
prepare a corresponding schedule and submit the same to the audit
team; and
b. Prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the issued semi-expandable
items as expense/accumulated surplus and the unissued property to its
related inventory accounts.

We further recommended that the Supply/Property Officer ensure that an
ICS was issued to the end-user and appropriately recorded in the stock
cards to establish accountability and monitor its whereabouts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Inventory committee to expedite the conduct of inventory taking and
render a report thereon in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.
b. Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records
and furnish the inventory committee a copy thereto to assist the
committee in the conduct of the physical count of the PPE.
c. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correction/adjustment in
the PPE account based on the inventory report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary reconciliations and submit to the audit
team for verification.

We recommended that management stop the grant of honoraria to national
government employees unless provided with an appropriation and until the
municipality has fully implemented the Modified Salary Standardization
Schedule and other conditions prescribed in the Salary Standardization
Law.

This audit recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Designate a Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the availability of cash
for the immediate release of AICS.
b. Instruct the MSWDO to prioritize the preparation of the assessment
report and immediately submit to the concerned officers to facilitate
the outright release of the AICS.
c. Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office
in formulating the implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid
or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation aligned
with the DSWD-Memorandum Circular No. 11 S of 2019.
d. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all AICS are supported with
complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
officials responsible in the procurement process to ensure that the LGU only
transact business with the legitimate suppliers/service providers. Likewise,
require the officials concerned to submit the lacking documents mentioned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install control and monitoring
mechanisms to ensure that the use of government vehicles and consumption
of fuel shall strictly be for a valid public purpose. Henceforth, the following
documents and reports should be required on the use of government
vehicles and fuel consumption:
a. Serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets approved by the Municipal
Mayor or his authorized representative to control the use of government
vehicle;
b. Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned
and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment
custodian summarizing in chronological order his trips or use of
equipment, respectively, for the month, duly certified by the driver and
approved by the Administrative Officer; and
c. Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly
to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the
different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the
month, total distance travelled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel
consumption for each vehicle.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Impose the forfeiture of the performance security and/or reimburse
from the contractor any amount that will be incurred to correct the
defects/damages found within the defect liability period.
b. Require the posting of the warranty security by the contractors after
the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the agency be
strictly enforced. To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the
LGU may make it a requirement to observe the one (1) year period
after completion of the project before returning the retention money
after which the warranty period shall commence;
c. Observe the period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the
imposition of the warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final
acceptance depending on the nature of the infrastructure project; and
d. Require the BAC to maintain a record and monitor the validity of
posted securities so that replacement thereof at times of expiration can
be demanded immediately.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a. Instruct the members of MIATF to ensure that environmental surfaces
in the evacuation center (quarantine) facility are regularly and
thoroughly cleaned, sanitized and maintained before, during and after
the quarantine period of the occupants.
b. Require the MPDC to regularly monitor the status/condition of the
infrastructure projects of the LGU and recommend appropriate actions
for its maintenance and/or repairs.
c. Ensure that all properties of the LGU are properly maintained,
managed and safeguarded against loss and wastage.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to create a well-planned detailed engineering
and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all aspect of
the construction/phases of the project to ensure that the POW shall
cover at least a usable portion of the project and to see to it that no
construction shall be started for portions of the project that are less than
usable;
b. The concerned offices to identify the reasons and address the hindrances
in the commencement of the work and start of the construction
works/phase for the completion of the project; and
c. The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work
(POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed
breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from
the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure
continuity of the works.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC, MENRO
and Municipal Engineer to coordinate and adequately plan the
procurement of infrastructure projects. Strategic locations of each type of
infrastructure projects should be considered, including the protection of
public health and environment.
Meanwhile, the management should refrain from using the evacuation
center as isolation/quarantine facility while the wastes/garbage has not
been relocated and disposed. Hence, prioritize the disposal of the wastes
and sanitization of the area. Further, alternative measures and/or use of the
MRF should be formulated so as not to put LGU funds to waste.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to improve collection strategies on RPT and initiate the available remedial measures in collecting the overdue taxes from delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant to compute/determine the amount of impairment and provide for allowance for impairment for dormant Other Receivables account in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under IPSAS 29; and (b) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts would be present, direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Legal Officer to initiate the procedures for the application of the request under COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee and designated GSO to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year and immediately submit the CY 2021 related reports to the Office of the Auditor; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the Accounting records; (b) Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO to maintain complete PPE records such as PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment; and (c) Municipal Accountant to transfer and recognize in the GF books all infrastructure assets recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continue its efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1; (b) require the LDRRMC to immediately implement the remaining PPAs to further strengthen the disaster preparedness and mitigation program of the Municipality; and (c) require the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the planning and reporting for LDRRMF PPAs as provided for under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 36(a) of R.A. No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (b) strictly comply with Section 36 of R.A. No. 9710 and PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided; and (c) maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were allocated.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) BAC to produce a complete and detailed APP showing the milestone procurement activities of the PAPs; (b) Municipal Engineer to closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure completion within the contract time for the immediate utilization of the intended beneficiaries; and (c) Local Development Council (LDC) through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to consider the immediate reprogramming to other priority undertakings of projects that determined to be unviable for implementation during the budget year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately submit all paid disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents and (b) henceforth, observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission; and (c) submit immediately the periodic trial balances and financial statements to the office of the Auditor for the months of January to November 2021.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the designated GSO/Property Officer to (a) prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018; and (b) obtain insurance for all insurable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to (i) submit immediately the copies of lacking documentary requirements for the above procurements; (ii) pursue the creation of the agency website and enhance the public disclosure of LGU’s procurements; and (iii) henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; and (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen his review function on the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) renew the appointment of the BAC and observe the requirements of Section 11.2.6 of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR. We also recommended that the LCE require the BAC to (a) ensure that the Municipality uses the latest Philippine Bidding Documents (PBDs) and forms to be submitted by the bidders in all of its procurement activities; (b) invite observers in all stages of the procurement process; (c) prepare the Minutes of Detailed Evaluation of Bids; and (d)  prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the form prescribed by the GPPB and observe its submission in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue to a) exert best efforts in implementing its COVID-19 Vaccination Program in view of the Philippine National Deployment and Vaccination Plan for COVID-19 Vaccine guidelines; and b) intensify the information campaign to the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine benefits to achieve the herd immunity as a way to fight the said virus.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 dated March 23, 2007 and July 8, 2008.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with the GSIS law on the deduction and remittance of the contributions and premiums due to the GSIS.

5.20	We commended Management for providing and utilizing necessary resources to respond to and eliminate the threat of COVID-19. However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to (i) determine the eligibility of the property procured whether the same falls under the category of PPE or just a semi-expendable property; (ii) take up inventories as such and not as an outright expense; and (iii) scrutinize carefully all the supporting documents attached to every disbursement.

6.8	We recommended that Management require the:
a.	Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end of the year for submission to the MAO; and
b.	Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for the restoration to the respective Cash in Bank account of the total amount of unreleased checks to arrive at the correct balance of the Cash and other related accounts.
6.9	During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer committed that the observation will be corrected for the next fiscal year since the lapses noted for CY 2021 were already been corrected by the issuances of the subject checks to the payees.

7.12	We recommended that Management require the:
General Services Officer/Storekeeper and concerned Offices (i) prepare and furnish the Accounting Office on a monthly basis the copies of SSMI supported with RIS and/or proof of distribution of goods and supplies; (ii) conduct a semestral physical count of inventories and report the results thereof in the RPCI; and (iii) reconcile and tally the RPCI with Inventory accounts in General Ledger; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct a thorough review of the Inventory accounts and reconcile with the Offices concerned; (ii) derecognize only in the books the items which were already consumed and/or distributed to end-users in a timely manner; and (iii) record all procurement of inventory supplies in the books using the Perpetual Inventory System.

9.11	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Health Officer and staff with the coordination of the Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the list of eligible frontliners duly approved by the Local Health Board, DTRs, duties, and responsibilities with accomplishment reports of regular, casuals and JO personnel and Appointments/plantilla for casuals, JOs and BHWs for us to evaluate the validity and propriety of the grant and disbursement of the AHDP and SRA.
9.12	We also recommended that Management submit a legal basis for the grant of AHDP to barangay and national government personnel in order to validate the legality and validity of payment thereof.

10.17	We recommended that Management:
advise the Municipal Accountant to (i) take up inventories as such and not as an outright expense; (ii) secure the necessary distribution report of all supplies and inventories prior to taking up the expense account; and (iii) scrutinize carefully all the supporting documents attached to every disbursement; and
require the General Services Offices and concerned offices to prepare and submit the SSMI based on the issued RIS or equivalent proofs of distribution of antigen test kits to the Accounting Office on a monthly basis and conduct a periodic physical count of inventories on hand.
10.18 The Municipal Accountant asserted her willingness to comply with the recommendations and discussed her planned detailed approach to the Audit Team during the exit conference. Proper categorization of PPE and inventory and the initial recognition of inventory were well taken noted by Management.

11.17	We recommended that Management:
direct the LDRRM Officer to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund and the desired benefits; and
require (i) the GSO to diligently prepare/maintain inventory records such as Stock Cards (SCs) and Property Cards (PCs), Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, RIS and Inventory List of all procured and donated items and coordinate with the Accounting Office for the periodic reconciliation of both inventory records; and (ii) provide storage/stockroom located in accessible, safe and secured areas that will accommodate all supplies, medicines, materials, equipment and relief goods of the MDRRMO to avoid possible loss and physical quality deterioration and can immediately response in times of calamities.
11.18 The LDRRMO was reoriented and briefed anew by Management during the exit conference as to the achievement of timely and full implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs. Recommendations regarding the Inventories procured out of the LDRRMF were well taken noted by Management.

12.9	We recommended that Management promptly implement PPAs financed by subject-fund that socio-economic benefits expected to produce by these projects will be enjoyed by constituents within the budget year and ensure continued economic development in the community.
12.10	Management assured the Audit Team that for the current year they were already on time because of their efforts to implement and practice the conduct of early procurement activities.

13.7	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the prescribed timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submittal of financial reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, we shall be constrained to enforce the provision of Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 on the withholding of salaries of all Municipal Officials deemed responsible for late/non-reporting of agency accounts.

14.23	We recommended that Management:
instruct the MPDC to facilitate the gathering of data/information needed for the creation of a well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable basis in the planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the policy formulation in order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD issues and concerns; and
facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system to help and assist the concerned officials in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs and the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same.
14.24  Management had already shown their compliance to the audit recommendation by working out the creation of the GAD M&E system. They also started setting up data based through their respective departments in the LGU.

15.15   We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Health and LDRRM Officers to conduct preliminary market scanning on prevailing prices of drugs and medicines subject to VAT exemptions granted to certain goods before preparing their PRs;
BAC including its Secretariat/Technical Working Group (TWG) to (i) consider/distinguish VAT-exempt items from those usually subject to 12% VAT in the determination of ABC and in the preparation of bid documents; and (ii) undertake detailed evaluation of financial component of bids to ensure that individual cost estimates submitted by participating bidders are in compliance with the existing SRPs and pertinent laws, rules, and regulations; and
General Services Officer/Storekeeper to ensure that VAT-exempt transactions are itemize/shown separately in sales invoice.
15.16	During the exit conference, Management averred that they already considered and shown those VAT-exempt items in the procurement documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Inventory Committee to allocate adequate time for the:
• completion of the necessary information and data to be reflected in the RPCPPE which will be used in reconciliation of the PPEs; and
• completion of the necessary reconciliation of the Property and Accounting Records in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to put their records of PPE and accounts in agreement, identify and investigate the causes of the discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments in records, if warranted.
2. Municipal Accountant to maintain the PPELC for each category of plant, property and equipment instead lapsing schedule.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disposal Committee to submit to the Audit Team the IIRUP, one for the already disposed unserviceable properties in CY 2019 and those for disposal based on the RPCPPE for CY 2020; to continue to focus on the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with existing regulations; and consequently, the Accounting Office to derecognize its value based on Paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the Notice of Disallowance No. 16-040-101(15) by debiting the Receivables – Disallowances and Charge account and by crediting the Prior Period Adjustment account for the amount of P125,000.00. 
Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowance pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor continually require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advance/s. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances.
The Municipal Mayor will discuss with other concerned department heads to address the issue and a possibility of additional Special Disbursing Officer (SDO).
observations.reason: Despite the regular reminders to accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period, there are still cash advances that were not liquidated immediately or as soon as its purpose have been served. For CY 2021, the number of cash advances that were not liquidated within the reglementary period was significantly lessened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send out confirmation letters to the payees as to validity of their balances and to request from them the pertinent documents to support their claims for processing and payment within a given period. In the event that the payees were unable to support their claims with the necessary documents within a given period, require the Municipal Accountant to revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality after securing an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and in compliance with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality after securing an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer or the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the intact deposit of daily or on the next banking day in compliance with Sections 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer continue to require the intact deposit of daily or on the next banking day in compliance with Sections 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. The Municipal Treasurer will discuss with other concerned department heads and consider additional SDO arrangements to address the issue.
observations.reason: There are instances that the depository bank is not operational for weeks due to COVID-19 pandemic and due to geographical location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount of P75,180.00 representing the unnecessary expense and henceforth, issue regularly update the cashbook and cash in bank for the proper timely monitoring of the balances of each account of the Municipality to avoid incurrence of unnecessary bank service charges for issuing checks with insufficient funds/cash balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refunded the amount of P75,180.00 and regularly update the cashbook and cash in bank for the proper monitoring of the account balances.
observations.reason: P75,180.00 was settled under Official Receipt No. 0432074 dated May 28, 2021 and deposited to the authorized depository bank on the same day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the –
(a)	GSO to 
(i)	maintain Property Cards starting with the current and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered; and 

(ii)	reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory-taking; 

(b)	Municipal Accountant to 
(i)	compute and take - up the depreciation expenses of the current year, update the related accumulated depreciation accounts as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for prior years’ depreciation expense which were not previously recorded;

(ii)	recognize the completed road project amounting to ₱10,894,773.25 as an addition to its Road Network Systems and Government Equity accounts in the General Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and 

(iii)	determine the projects transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure for 100% recognition in the appropriate asset accounts; and 

(c)	Inventory Committee to undertake complete physical inventory of all property of the municipality, prepare complete and detailed report thereon and reconcile the result with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 
(a) Acting Municipal Treasurer to 
(i)	maintain updated Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR); and 

(ii)	prepare and submit a certified list of taxpayer and amount due thereof at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant; 

(b) Municipal Accountant to recognize RPT Receivables and SET Receivables based on the said certified list of taxpayer and tax due submitted by the Acting Municipal Treasurer.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of the fund to the EMB to facilitate proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 
(a)	Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the claims; and 

(b)	ensure that funds are utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officials in charge of the relief distribution to submit distribution list, and/or justification/explanation to establish the accountability of concerned Municipal government officials/employees on the goods distributed.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to 
(a)	allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 36 (a) of RA 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; 

(b)	strictly comply with Section 36 of RA 9710 and PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the 

intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided; and 

(c)	maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWD Officer and Acting General Services officer to comply with the guidelines on the proper accounting and reporting of the receipt, distribution and balances of in-kind donations in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2020-009 and 2014-002 dated April 21, 2020 and April 15, 2014, respectively.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Property Custodian to 
(a)	prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018; and 

(b)	obtain insurance for all insurable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management 
(a)	consider reducing the number of bank deposit accounts maintained by the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to what is required and necessary only; and 

(b)	submit justification why some bank deposit accounts of the Municipality were not maintained in the nearest authorized government depository bank in the area.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within the prescribed period in accordance with the GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the preparation and submission of the Updated/Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with the provisions of GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to 
(a)	continue to adhere to the requirements of RA 8291; and 

(b)	submit the Journal Entry Voucher and its supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue deducting and remitting all contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue adherence to the provisions of RA 9679.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the –
(a) GSO to (i) maintain Property Cards starting with the current and recent
years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE
items are covered; and (ii) reconcile inventory results with property
records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items
not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory-taking;
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) compute and take - up the depreciation
expenses of the current year, update the related accumulated
depreciation accounts as well as record adjustments in the government
equity account for prior years’ depreciation expense which were not
previously recorded; (ii) recognize the completed road project amounting
to ₱10,894,773.25 as an addition to its Road Network Systems and
Government Equity accounts in the General Fund books in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (iii)
determine the projects transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure
for 100% recognition in the appropriate asset accounts; and
(c) Inventory Committee to undertake complete physical inventory of all
property of the municipality, prepare complete and detailed report
thereon and reconcile the result with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Acting
Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain updated Real Property Tax Account Register
(RPTAR); and (ii) prepare and submit a certified list of taxpayer and amount due
thereof at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to recognize RPT Receivables and SET Receivables based on the said
certified list of taxpayer and tax due submitted by the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to –
(a) cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU
officials/employees; and
(b) ensure that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA
Auditor for review. The necessary documents must be attached therein, such
as the official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, etc.
Henceforth, introduce controls that would assure that cash advances are
liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to
comply with the guidelines on the proper accounting and reporting of the receipt
and utilization of cash donations in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002
dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of the fund to the EMB to facilitate
proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and personnel concerned to submit the required documents
immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Acting
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required
documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of
the claims; and (b) ensure that funds are utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officials in
charge of the relief distribution to submit distribution list, and/or
justification/explanation to establish the accountability of concerned Municipal
government officials/employees on the goods distributed.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) allocate at
least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities
as required under Section 36 (a) of RA 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (b)
strictly comply with Section 36 of RA 9710 and PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
2013-01 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that
the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other
assistance that may be provided; and (c) maximize the utilization of the GAD funds
through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to
attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWD Officer
and Acting General Services officer to comply with the guidelines on the proper
accounting and reporting of the receipt, distribution and balances of in-kind
donations in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2020-009 and
2014-002 dated April 21, 2020 and April 15, 2014, respectively.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Property Custodian to (a) prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF)
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018; and (b) obtain
insurance for all insurable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management (a) consider reducing the number of
bank deposit accounts maintained by the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to what is
required and necessary only; and (b) submit justification why some bank deposit
accounts of the Municipality were not maintained in the nearest authorized
government depository bank in the area.

of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the submission of the Procurement
Monitoring Report (PMR) within the prescribed period in accordance with the
GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Head
of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the preparation and submission of the
Updated/Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with the provisions
of GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and
intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum
Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.

Municipal Accountant to (a) continue to adhere to the requirements of RA 8291;

Audit Team for verification.

withheld to the PhilHealth.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to continue adherence to the provisions of RA 9679.

require the Municipal Budget Officer to issue approved Obligation Request before the awarding of the contract as basis of the Accounting Office to certify the availability of funds; and
require the Municipal Engineering Office and other officials concerned to disclose all relevant information about the PPAs in the signboards in order to promote transparency and accountability.

direct the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) properly coordinate each other, including MDRRMC, to avoid error in the preparation of LDRRMF Utilization Report to incorporate those PPAs obligated near the end of the year; (ii) submit the required documents of Check No. 919696 dated March 27, 2020 to the Office of the Auditor; and (iii) ensure that all supporting documents are provided with complete information for further evaluation of the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE to:

coordinate with the other LGU officials concerned, establish linkage of the existing CBMS with the GAD sex-aggregated data or GAD database to catalyze performance-based gender responsive planning on the basis of a reliable information system.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to undertake the following courses of action:

name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government and establish
ownership over them;
 continually monitor the accomplishment of the Municipal Assessor to ensure that

record the Notice of Disallowance No. 16-040-101(15) by debiting the Receivables –

account for the amount of P125,000.00.

written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the

officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances within the

send out confirmation letters to the payees as to validity of their balances and to request

payment within a given period. In the event that the payees were unable to support their

Accountant to revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of
the Municipality subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a
Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims
arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and in
compliance with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of
the account in the financial statements.

Treasurer to refund the amount of P75,180.00 representing the unnecessary expense

monitoring of the balances of each account of the Municipality to avoid incurrence of
unnecessary bank service charges for issuing checks with insufficient funds/cash bank
balance.

same may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within

2014-002 and 2020-009.

individual bank accounts maintained in the Authorized Government
Depository Bank;

the tool of the accountant in the preparation of the bank reconciliation
statement;
c. Examine the reconciling items and prepare journal entry vouchers for the
necessary adjustments in the books of the LGU.

b. Reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate asset accounts;
and

006 and submit a copy of the RPCPPE/Inventory Report not later than

c. Municipal Accountant to furnish the Auditor with subsidiary schedules

of the computation of the depreciation expenses of such assets; and
d. Municipal Accountant to secure the necessary documents and prepare
adjusting entries to book-up the unrecorded motor vehicle donated by
DOH and its related accumulated depreciation as well as adjust the
excessive depreciation charged against the Construction and Heavy
Equipment account.

the perpetual inventory method;

cards and stock cards; Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms;
c) Municipal Accountant to ensure coordination between the end-users and
the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper
accounting for the inventories;
d) Record the expenditures to its appropriate inventory and/or expenses
accounts

to provide additional information to all accounts in the notes, especially those

nature, composition or any changes thereto pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS
Volume I and Section 111 of PD 1445, to provide readers with the information
necessary for the full understanding of the operation of the LGU.

instruct the LCPC/MCPWPC to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of laws, policy rules and regulations (e.g. DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2008-126, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120, R.A. No. 9344, R.A. 8980, etc.)
direct the Municipal Budget Office to observe the rules on the mandatory allocation of one (1%) of IRA to the LGU’s Annual Budget supporting LCPC/MCWPC programs, projects and activities through issuance of an ordinance; and
establish a functional Inter-Agency Monitoring Task Force to evaluate and monitor the effectiveness of LCPC/CCWPC PPAs.
Advise the Municipal Council for the Welfare and Protection of the Children to:
to prepare and submit required documents e.g. Annual Work and Financial Plan duly approved by the Sanggunian concerned, Approved Action Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC); Local State of Children Report (LSCR), Annual Accomplishment Report/ Annual Report and other reports as mentioned above; and
update the Local Code for Children, if necessary.
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the GFPS to (i) secure/submit Certificate of Review and Endorsement as proof of approval of GAD PB and comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women, as amended by JMC No. 2016 – 01 and use of Guide on the Review and Endorsement of LGUs GAD Plan and Budget and Review of LGU’s GAD Accomplishment Report; and (ii) recommend and plan an appropriate capacity building program on GAD, like training on the technical aspect of GAD mainstreaming of LGU’s PAPs and use of Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines/Tools to ensure that GFPS are effectively capacitated on GAD processes and mainstreaming of the GAD PPAs;
in coordination with the other LGU officials concerned, establish linkage of the existing CBMS with the GAD sex-aggregated data or GAD database as required under JMC No. 2013 – 01 to catalyze performance-based gender responsive planning on the basis of a reliable information system; and
establish GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to support the effectiveness of GAD PPAs and consider the issuance of an Executive Order or Administrative Order to strengthen the M&E System.
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to
allocate adequate time for the:
• continuation and completion of the conduct of physical count of all properties and
preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and
data; and
• completion of the reconciliation of the Property and Accounting Records in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant to put their records of PPE and accounts
in agreement, identify and investigate the causes of the noted discrepancies, and
effect the necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disposal Committee to
continue to focus on the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in
accordance with existing regulations; and consequently, the Accounting Office to
derecognize its value based on Paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of
the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary validation and adjustments of the long outstanding deposits in
transit of P25,279.44, correct the erroneous adjusting entry made for the DIT
amounting to P40,015.28 in General Fund and to effect the adjustment in SEF, and
adjust the remaining 27 stale checks of P167,455.42 to establish the correct
balances of the Cash in Bank Local Currency – Current Account for the fair
presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Assessor to continually facilitate the titling of the other
untitled lots in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the
government and establish ownership over them;
• continually monitor the accomplishment of the Municipal Assessor to ensure that
subject lots are titled at least within the year and that funds for the purpose is
continuously provided;
• require the Appraisal Committee to conduct the appraisal of the donated lots for
recording purposes in the books of accounts; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to identify the recorded land in the Special
Education Fund for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial
statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continually:
• require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their
cash advances within the reglementary period on a regular basis or as soon as the
purpose of the cash advance has been served, preferably upon completion of the
project/program/ activity; and
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to continually send written notices or
demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle their cash advances,
otherwise, implement the withholding of their salary to be applied to his
accounts.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjustment in the Other Payables account and to reflect the correct
entries of the recognition of receivables and settlement of disallowances on the basis of
complete supporting documents in the Receivables-Disallowances and Charges account
of P1,582,432.58 for its fair presentation in the financial statements as required under
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
submit the proof of deposit for the audit disallowances with issued Official Receipts for
further verification of the Audit Team and possible issuance of Notice of Settlement of
Suspension, Disallowance and Charges.

Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce
the immediate settlement of the disallowance pursuant to the pertinent provisions
of COA Circular No. 2009-06.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
consider strategies that would allow him to manage his time in the discharge of his
regular functions, among others, the strict observance of depositing intact his
collections and those turned over to him by other collecting officers daily or on the
next banking day in compliance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I,
to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to refund the amount of P52,780.00 representing the unnecessary expense
and henceforth, regularly update the cashbook and cash in bank for the proper and
timely monitoring of the balances of each account of the Municipality to avoid incurrence
of unnecessary bank service charges for issuing checks with insufficient funds/cash
bank balance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) locate or secure copy of documents from the creditors concerned to prove the validity of the accounts; b) make necessary reconciliation/adjustment, if there is any, and to strictly adhere to sound accounting practices/standards in the keeping of accounts as provided for in, Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: Documentation still not secured
Long outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Local DRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to effect the submission of the required reports stated under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and (b) Head of the implementing unit to inform the audit team of the posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in the aforementioned circular for validation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006 on the submission of copy of POs/contracts and delivery receipts within the reglementary period to facilitate the timely review and inspection of deliveries.

We recommended that Management perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure intact remittance of the GSIS compulsory contribution within the first ten days of the following month as required in Section 52(c) of RA No. 8291.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue adherence to the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9679.

observations.managementAction:

All the unused funds were returned to the source agencies (SAs).





Remittances of unutilized amount of all completed projects were made.
observations.reason:
Not all unaccounted balances were remitted.




Incomplete supporting documents were still noted.




Lack of some documentary requirements was still noted.



There were still BuB projects that remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of relief goods purchased out of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stopped the practice of taking up in the books as outright expenses those that should be taken as inventory under the Perpetual Inventory Method.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the receivables from the unpaid space rentals to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the Agency’s Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant took up the receivables in the books.

observations.recommendation:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) scrutinize the completeness of supporting documents of claims before releasing the same for payment; and (ii) request for the replacement of invalid official receipt issued by the supplier of meals during the Municipal Athletic Meet; and

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit complete documentation on the procurement of netbooks, overhead projectors and workbooks for students.
observations.managementAction:
The utilization of the SEF was continuously maximized.




Replacement of invalid official receipt was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fulfill faithfully their obligation to remit the barangay shares from RPT collections within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remitted the barangay shares from RPT collections.

Priority development PPAs partaking the nature of capital expenditures were funded out of the 20% DF.




Posting of the Invitation to Bid and other Notices in the PhilGEPS was already complied with.




Management conducted pre-procurement conference





The BAC invited representa-tives from COA and other observers.
observations.reason: Lack of some supporting documents was still noted.

observations.managementAction:
The barangays actively participated in the campaign on Solid Waste Management.





The implemen-tation of the “No segregation, No collection” policy was monitored and implemented.




Containers with appropriate markings were required in every business establish-ment and barangay.




Continuous transport of residual waste was made with Metro Clark.

Close coordination was made with DENR but the location for the sanitary landfill was not yet feasible.




No financial assistance was obtained from the National Agencies despite effort exerted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management stop the practice of designating non-permanent employee to a position exercising power or capacity to control certain operations which is more suited to a permanent or regular employee. 

Likewise, instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate and assess the need of creating a plantilla position for MENRO, subject to availability of funds.
observations.reason:
Some Job order personnel were made in charge of duties assigned to a supervisory position.



Some Casual and J.O. personnel were considered to fill up a permanent position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management (a) adhere to the required composition of the GAD Focal Point System, and the creation of GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) and Monitoring and Evaluation Team to institutionalize gender mainstreaming; and 


(b) submit updated GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment and Utilization Report to provide basis of measuring the performance of the LGU in terms of all the requirements of the PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction:

GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplish-ment and Utilization Report were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • direct the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of the additional inventory of some PPE and prepare the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and data; • instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of PPE and accounts and effect necessary adjustments; • require the Disposal Committee to immediately dispose unserviceable properties in accordance with the Manual on Disposal and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP); and consequently, the Accounting Office to derecognize its value based on Paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was able to conduct additional inventories of some PPE such as the Road Networks, Buildings, Markets, Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment, Medical Equipment and Motor Vehicles. The inventory was evidenced by the preparation and submission of the RPCPPE. The Municipal Accountant adjusted the balance of the Buildings account to reflect the appropriate amount of the renovation made in the municipal buildings. The Disposal Committee was able to collate the unserviceable properties and prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP).
observations.reason: a) absence of a prescribed format of the RPCPPE was still not complied. b) partial inventory count performed by the Inventory Committee. c) disposal have not yet been undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to identify the validity of the deposits in transit and the stale checks that were causing the reconciling items and to appropriately adjust the Bank Reconciliation Statement and the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The reconciling items from CY 2018 of P1,296,420.91 significantly decreased due to adjustments made in CY 2019 by the Municipal Accountant totaling P1,103,686.05 leaving a balance of reconciling items totaling P192,734.86. LBP Account No. 2612-1025-73 (General Fund) totaling P153,340.49 was correctly adjusted thru JEV Nos. 902-905. LBP Account No. 2612-1029-72 (Special Education Fund) totaling P40,015.28 was erroneously adjusted in the General Fund instead of SEF thru JEV Nos. 2031 and 2032. DBP Account No. 0620-13517-030 (GF) amounting to P25,279.44 was not validated and adjusted in CY 2019. Of 63 stale checks totaling P1,077,785.70 in CY 2018, P910,330.28 representing 36 stale checks were adjusted in CY 2019. The remaining 27 stale checks amounting to P167,455.42 were not adjusted in CY 2019 due to further verification against the corresponding disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Erroneous adjustment made in LBP Account No. 2612-1029-72; non-adjustment of DBP Account No. 0620-13517-030; and pending adjustment for the 27 stale checks as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period or as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served, preferably upon completion of the project/program/activity; and instruct the Municipal Accountant to continually send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle their cash advances, otherwise, implement the withholding of their salary to be applied to his accounts. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant forwards certifications of unliquidated cash advances from time to time to the Mayor’s Office who will be the one to send written notices or demands to the concerned accountable officers. An Assistant Municipal Treasurer assumed office in CY 2019 who was assigned as a special disbursing officer aside from the Municipal Treasurer which helped in improving the liquidation of CAs within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Additional CAs and some long outstanding CAs still remained outstanding; liquidation improved but not all CAs liquidated within reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the untitled lots in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government and establish ownership over them; • monitor the accomplishment of the Municipal Assessor to ensure that subject lots are titled within the year and that funds for the purpose are continuously provided; and • require the Appraisal Committee to conduct the appraisal of the donated lots for recording purposes in the books of accounts; and • require the Municipal Accountant to identify the recorded land in the Special Education Fund for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was able to process and title four (4) titles for CY 2019. The Appraisal Committee will conduct the appraisal of the remaining unrecorded lots to facilitate its recording in the books of accounts. Management exerted efforts in locating the valid documents pertaining to the land recorded in SEF.
observations.reason: Processing of remaining titles may take longer due to limited operations of government agencies (COVID-19). Municipal Accountant still not able to identify recorded land in SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to record and update all the transactions in the cashbooks, certify the correctness of the cashbook balances every end of the month and maintain and present them to the Audit Team Leader. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to locate the cashbook for DBP Account No. 620-24601-030 to determine the cause of the negative balance reflected in the Subsidiary Ledger.
observations.managementAction: Cashbooks were updated. Periodic reconciliation was performed by the Municipal Accounting Staff and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant adjusted negative balance in the Subsidiary Ledger.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to consider strategies that would allow him to manage his time in the discharge of his regular functions, among others, the strict observance of depositing intact his collections and those turned over to him by other collecting officers daily or on the next banking day in compliance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: Management commented they are already strategizing the manner of collection and deposits in order to lessen the recurrence of time lags. They added that since the assumption of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer the time lags already improved.
observations.reason: Delay in deposits ranged and recurrence of time lags though improved after assignment of Assistant Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to assess the municipal buildings for insurance coverage with the GSIS and require the Municipal Treasurer to act on the insurance of the buildings as well as the heavy equipment with the GSIS.

The GSIS Representative requested for the submission of the building plans from the Engineering Office which were not yet submitted. The wheel loader is already for disposal being unserviceable for a long time, while the delivery truck is already insured in 2019. During the year the Municipality paid P69,721.25 for insurance of three ambulance, two municipal vehicle and one heavy equipment.
observations.reason: Building plans required by GSIS not yet submitted; some heavy equipment remain uninsured or for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated MENRO to: • revise the Solid Waste Management Plan and seek its approval by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval in compliance with RA No. 9003; and • complete the Environmental Impact and Management Plan and the Geohazard Identification Report required by the EMB for their ECC.
observations.managementAction: The Designated MENRO attended seminars on WACS and conducted WACS on the Municipality's barangays and is incorporating the same in the 10-year SWM plan. The MRF for the 20 barangays was established. For the sanitary landfill, 16 out of 18 documents required by the EMB for ECC were submitted; Environmental Impact and Management Plan and Geohazard Identification Report were still for compliance and submission.
observations.reason: WACS being incorporated and plan revision ongoing; ECC requirements still pending completion of required documents (Environmental Impact and Geohazard reports).

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to strictly observe the prescribed timeline in the submission of contract and supporting documents which is five (5) days from its execution/ perfection.

observations.reason: Continued refusal to adhere as required shall subject concerned officials to legal consequences provided by law.

observations.managementAction: The position of Municipal Treasurer was applied for the vacant position.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended that management implement immediately the unimplemented projects under the 20% DF and henceforth, implement all development projects, consistent with the general policy of the government and mandated responsibility of LGU officials as embodied in the Joint DBM- DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, in order to attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Please see related discussion on Finding No. 9 of this report

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommended for the LCE to instruct concerned officials to provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the Municipality’s total annual appropriation as required in DBM- NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Section 30 of the General Appropriations Act of FY 2018. 5. We further recommend to fully utilize the funds. Likewise, the Municipality should prepare and submit the Accomplishment Report showing among others, the amount utilized in the implementation of projects lined up in the GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction: The management has already submitted its Accomplishment Report.

If feasible, assign additional personnel to assist the designated Solid Waste Management Officer who also functions as the Municipal Agricultural Officer; and 8. Integrate in the Annual Investment Plan the construction of the Sanitary Landfill and classify as urgent. The construction of the sanitary landfill is one among the identified projects that is chargeable to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: To address the issues, we recommended the following: The Municipal Mayor to convene the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to create the SWMP and for its implementation; assign additional personnel; integrate in the AIP the construction of the Sanitary Landfill.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: Please see related discussion on Finding No. 11 of this report

The Local Chief Executive with the help of the Municipal Budget Officer create three (3) staff in compliance with RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to undertake the setting of direction, development, and planning on the implementation and coordination of disaster risk management program; 10. The LDRRMO make a Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) and submit the same to the auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; 11. The LCE through the LDRRM Office to maximize the use of the funds for the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities for prevention and mitigation and promote disaster preparedness; and 12. The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries and effect the actual transfer of the remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund to a special trust fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to take necessary actions to ensure the implementation of the projects subject of the fund transfers as mandated in the Joint Memorandum Circular. If on the other hand, the LGU is unable to implement the projects, the funds received should immediately be returned to the donor agency so it can be channeled to other government agencies where it can be put into use instead of being dormant in depository banks.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) and members of the Local School Board (LSB) to prepare the Annual SEF Budget following the procedure set forth in the Joint Circular and furnish the Local Accountant, Local Treasurer and Local Budget a copy of the approved budget in the prescribed format; 15. The LCE observe the policies and guidelines contained in the joint circular to ensure a strategic and efficient utilization of resources for priority programs and projects; and 16. The LCE to ensure that all public schools within its jurisdiction should equally avail of the SEF for the benefit of all students.
observations.managementAction: The LGU submitted SEF budget.

observations.recommendation: 17. We recommend the Municipal Treasurer properly observe the requirements under DV and COA Circular No. 92-389 in order to maintain a sound internal control in the utilization of government funds. The perforation of these documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but should include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which shall provide a space for the number of the check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces should be properly filled up.
observations.managementAction: Management to ensure supporting documents are stamped paid.

Prudent use of the scarce financial resources of the government should be observed in sending municipal officials and employees to conventions, seminars and similar activities.
observations.managementAction: Management will review and ensure compliance.

observations.managementAction: Bank Statements for the SEF and Trust Fund were prepared and submitted.

observations.managementAction: The LGU conducted orientation for the DRM reports but submission remains incomplete.
