In order to achieve the inclusive growth and poverty reduction as envisioned by the Bottom-up Budgeting Program, we recommend that management should expedite the implementation of the projects in accordance with Section 5 of the DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; LGU to expedite implementation.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books
for the stale checks to avoid its repetitive inclusion in the subsequent BRS.

1.10 We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the BRS.

2.10 We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

2.10.1 Instruct the officers and employees concerned for the immediate
settlement of their outstanding cash advances;
2.10.2 Withhold the salaries of the accountable officers until they make
liquidation of their cash advances;
2.10.3 Send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the
same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate the filing
of cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445;
2.10.4 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the grant of additional
cash advance unless the previous cash advance given to the officers
and employees is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made;
and
2.10.5 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue a certification that the
employee has no outstanding cash advance for every cash advance
granted.

3.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

3.19.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services
Officer, and Engineer to comply with the Accounting and
Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System,
to perform their mandated duties and responsibilities in the
submission of reports and maintenance of records, and to
coordinate with each other.

3.19.2 Submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE
(RCPPE), and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card
(PPELC)

3.19.3 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw
Journal Entry Vouchers to transfer the completed projects to the
General Fund, and to correct the errors in the books of the LGU
relative to the computation of depreciation.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal
Accountant with a duly certified list of amount due and collectible for the year
based on the Real Property Tax Account Register at the start of every year.

4.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to establish the correct amount of
receivables and draw adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

a) Record regular purchases of inventories using the perpetual inventory
method;
b) Conduct physical count and submit report on inventories on hand
twice a year;
c) Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger
cards and stock cards;
d) Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; and
e) Ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office
for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting for
the inventories

6.12 We recommended to require all the personnel concerned, especially the
municipal accountant, to attach complete documentations on all disbursements,
to ensure the validity as well as correctness of the transactions. Significantly,
management thru the LCE and Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to
the policy of no payment without complete documentation.

6.13 We also recommended for the submission of the lacking supporting documents.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension as a result of
our audit.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to remit in full the taxes withheld on compensation and
payments to contractors and suppliers on or before the due dates.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the Revenue Collection
Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their collections
on a daily basis regardless of the nature of collections made.

8.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her
collections, as well as collections turned over to her by the collectors, at least
once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00, pursuant to Section
69(1) of PD 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary Program of
Work for the nine unimplemented projects;
b) Advise the Municipal Engineer to be diligent in the performance of her
duties in providing engineering services to the LGU, including
investigation and survey, engineering designs, and project management,
among others; and
c) Coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator and other officials of the LGU to ensure full utilization of
the 20% DF appropriated for each year.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal
entries to correct the erroneous entries.

10.8 Henceforth, he is advised to conform with the provisions of IPSAS and be
properly guided with the descriptions of the accounts so that the correct accounts
will be used for each transaction, and prevent the misstatement of each account.

11.9 We recommended that management should strictly comply with the provisions
of RA 9003 in the establishment of a sanitary landfill and in the formulation of
necessary education promotion and information campaign strategies on the
source reduction, segregation, recycling, composting of solid wastes, and the
establishment of a Sanitary Landfill.

12.9 We recommended that management fill in the plantilla positions, or designate
permanent staffs in concurrent capacity, or reassign LGU personnel to the
LDRRMO as required by the existing regulations who will perform the functions
of administration and training, research and planning and operations and
warning.

13.10 We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council to procure the basic equipage prescribed by DILG to
ensure that the municipality is prepared for calamities as prescribed in
NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

14.10 To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor,
HRMO, Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant:

a. Provide in the contract of service the expected service to be rendered
and funding charges;
b. Furnish the audit team copies of the Job Order Contracts/Service
Contracts within five (5) days after its approval. Ensure that it states
the expected output/service to be rendered, duration of work, office of
assignment, source of funds and its daily rate; and
c. Require each JO worker to prepare the accomplishment report,
stating therein the actual and specific tasks undertaken during the
period, duly verified and signed by the immediate supervisor, and
attached to the Payroll with the Daily Time Record.

15.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to observe the due dates of the
remittances of premium and loan amortization to Pag-IBIG.

16.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare three copies of the
Reports of Disbursement to liquidate her cash advance for each nature of cash
advance.

16.8 We also recommended that the Human Resource Officer submit to the
Treasurer’s Office/Accounting Office complete copies of Applications for
leave, travel documents and Minutes of Sessions attended/conducted and
Resolutions prepared/passed for elective officials for each pay period in
support of the payrolls for salaries and wages.

17.13 Hence, we recommended that the LGU management:

17.13.1 Strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, and
training of personnel responsible for planning and
implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that they are aligned with
GAD-related undertakings, so that the intended beneficiaries are
assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may
be provided.

17.13.2 Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code
or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the
localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is
mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local
development plans.

18.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial
transactions together with the supporting documents to the Accounting
Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to
COA on or before the 10th day of the following month.

18.10 We also recommended that the management should devise a process which
eliminates delays and repetition of work, which would promote efficient flow
of transactions and timely submission of accounts and report to the audit team.

18.11 Likewise, the accountable officers and/or other officials concerned are
reminded of the sanctions imposed under Section 122 of PD 1445 in relation to
Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment
of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two (2) months.

19.7 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified Persons
Responsible and Liable in the Notices suspension and Notices of Disallowance,
respectively to settle their audit suspension or disallowance.

19.8 We recommended further for the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the
Notices to the Persons Responsible/Liable for their information on their
unsettled suspensions/disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-General Service Office to procure its common-use supplies from the PS – DBM in compliance with Executive Order 17 and Section 53(5) of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  In case of non-availability of items in the PS-DBM, we recommend that procurement of these common use goods should be in bulk quantities to obtain the most advantageous price.

We reiterated our earlier recommendation that Management instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders/contacts and supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance/perfection to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately transmit the unsubmitted accounts covering the period November to December 2017 and the quarterly SAAOB for 2017, respectively; and henceforth adhere strictly to the prescribed timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submittal of financial reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Submission of accounts, financial reports and SAAOB  was still delayed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the analysis and documentation of the unremitted GSIS contribution by reconciling her records with that Agency and remit the same once the correctness of amount and details have been established as prescribed by R.A. No. 8291.
observations.reason: The unaccounted balance remained undetermined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the analysis and documentation of the unremitted Pag-IBIG contribution by reconciling her records with that Agency and remit the same once the correctness of amount and details have been established as prescribed by R.A. No. 9679.
observations.reason: The unaccounted balance remained undetermined.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holders of checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance, make the necessary adjustments for the stale checks and for long outstanding deposits in transit to establish the reliability of the Cash in Bank - Local Currency Account, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was unable to notify the payees of the checks for the stale checks. With the new development, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 2, page 36, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The reconciling items totaling P1,296,420.91 which include long outstanding deposits in transit and stale checks remained unadjusted. The Municipal Treasurer failed to send notices; stale checks accumulated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:  review and compare his cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items; and  henceforth, reconcile his cashbook with the accounting records at least quarterly to avoid accumulation of discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook entries of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are still in the process of reconciliation due to existence of discrepancies for both records.
observations.reason: With the new development, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 5, page 41, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact and daily or on the next banking day all collections, in compliance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was unable to deposit intact daily or the next banking day due to voluminous workload and the retirement of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Mayor informed the Audit Team that they have made a written request with the Landbank of the Philippines Satellite Office located beside the Municipal Building, to accept their deposits daily in order to comply with the recommendation on the prompt deposit of collections.
observations.reason: Delays increased from 2 to 16 in CY 2017 to 2 to 38 in CY 2018 due to retirement of Assistant Municipal Treasurer and voluminous workload; exposing funds to possible loss or misuse. Reiterated the audit observation under Observation No. 7, page 45, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount of P52,780.00 representing the unnecessary expenses and henceforth, regularly update the cashbook for cash in bank for the proper and timely monitoring of the balances of each account of the Municipality to avoid incurrence of unnecessary bank service charges for issuing checks with insufficient funds/cash bank balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor assured the Audit Team of the Municipal Treasurer’s compliance with the recommendation and that arrangements will be made to refund the amount.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to prioritize payment of the bank charges; bank charges incurred were due to issuance of checks without monitoring of available balance. Reiterated the audit observation under Observation No. 8, page 46, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed with the recommendation to insure all insurable PPEs with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Due to absence of assessment required by the GSIS, the Municipality was still unable to process the insurance of all insurable properties. With the new development, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 9, page 49, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit  timely the  Bank Reconciliation Statements as required in Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section  3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated  October 2, 1996 and to draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL as mandated in Sections 3.3  of the same COA Circular.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all executive officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided under Section 444.b.1.X of RA 7160.  Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to render their accounts, and all official and employees concerned to submit the other financial reports within the reglementary period.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit a timely and complete Bank Reconciliation Statement as required in Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011  and to draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL as mandated in Section 3.3 of the same COA Circular.

We recommend that management strictly observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39(1) and (3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not observed the timely submission of accounts and reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with Certified list of Taxpayers and tax due at the beginning of the year; and
Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Sec. 20 of NGAS in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of each year based on the Certified list of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for the account Due to LGUs; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to remit the province and barangays shares of real properties and other taxes in accordance with Section 286 of R.A. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) locate or secure copy of documents from the creditors concerned to prove the validity of the accounts; b) make necessary reconciliation/adjustment, if there is any, and to strictly adhere to sound accounting practices/standards in the keeping of accounts as provided for in, Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the:
department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008;
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer in the preparation of the LRNLC and include in the disclosure the total road networks in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
General Services Officer to (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inventory of local road network yearly; (ii) prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System, Report on Local Road Network, and (iii) maintain the Road Map and LRNPC for all roads and its components.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to secure proper documentation of the Land where the Municipal Hall Building is erected in order to obtain an insurance for the same including other structures therein.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Local DRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to effect the submission of the required reports stated under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and (b) Head of the implementing unit to inform the audit team of the posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in the aforementioned circular for validation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC Chairman to prepare the PMR to be approved by the Head of Procuring Entity before the same is submitted to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006 on the submission of copy of POs/contracts and delivery receipts within the reglementary period to facilitate the timely review and inspection of deliveries.

We recommended that Management perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure intact remittance of the GSIS compulsory contribution within the first ten days of the following month as required in Section 52(c) of RA No. 8291.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue adherence to the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9679.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of relief goods purchased out of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the receivables from the unpaid space rentals to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the Agency’s Financial Statements.

We recommended and management agreed to consider the following courses of actions:
Continue to maximize the utilization of the SEF and implement programs and projects, especially the improvements of school buildings and classrooms to ensure the provision of facilities conducive for learning;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) scrutinize the completeness of supporting documents of claims before releasing the same for payment; and (iii) request for the replacement of invalid official receipt issued by the supplier of meals during the Municipal Athletic Meet; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit complete documentation on the procurement of netbooks, overhead projectors and workbooks for students as detailed in Appendix G.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fulfill faithfully their obligation to remit the barangay shares from RPT collections within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that management stop the practice of designating non-permanent employee to a position exercising power or capacity to control certain operations which is more suited to a permanent or regular employee. Likewise, instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate and assess the need of creating a plantilla position for MENRO, subject to availability of funds.

We recommended that management (a) adhere to the required composition of the GAD Focal Point System, and the creation of GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) and Monitoring and Evaluation Team to institutionalize gender mainstreaming; and (b) submit updated GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment and Utilization Report to provide basis of measuring the performance of the LGU in terms of all the requirements of the PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.

We recommended that management require the accountable officers to settle their outstanding audit suspensions and/or disallowances through compliance with the requirements, or payment/restitution thereof or by any of the modes of extinguishment of obligation provided by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of the physical count
of all properties, exert all efforts to complete the inventory list of all existing
PPE and prepare the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary
information and data;
 instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile
their records of PPE accounts, identify and investigate the causes of the noted
discrepancies in the balances of accounts to bring the two records in
agreement; and effect necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted;
 require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the balance of the Buildings
account to reflect the appropriate amount of the renovation of the Municipal
Building; and
 initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance
with the Manual on Disposal by the Commission on Audit; and consequently
the Accounting Office to derecognize its value based on Paragraph 82 of
PPSAS 17 for fair presentation of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to identify the validity of the deposits in transit in order
to reflect appropriate adjustment in the Bank Reconciliation Statement including
the adjustment of stale checks and consequently establish the reliability of the
Cash in Bank - Local Currency Account, Current Account.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer notify the payees or holders
of checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale
so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these
issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all
accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances
within the reglementary period on a regular basis or as soon as the purpose of the
CA has been served, preferably upon completion of the project/program/activity.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officer to immediately settle
his cash advances, otherwise, implement the withholding of his salary to be
applied to his accounts.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the untitled donated
parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the
government and establish ownership over them;
 monitor the accomplishment of the Municipal Assessor to ensure that subject
lots are titled within the targeted time-frame and that funds for the purpose is
continuously provided; and
 require the Municipal Accountant to identify the recorded land in SEF for the
fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
record and update all the transactions in the cashbooks, certify the correctness of
the cashbook balances every end of the month and maintain and present them to
the Audit Team Leader.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
to locate the cashbook for the DBP Acct No. 620-24601-030 to determine the cause
of the negative balance reflected in the Subsidiary Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the immediate
settlement of the disallowances pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA
Circular 2009-06.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit intact and daily or on the next banking day all collections, in
compliance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid possible
loss or misuse of funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to refund the amount of P52,780.00 representing the unnecessary
expenses and henceforth, regularly update the cashbook for cash in bank for the
proper and timely monitoring of the balances of each account of the Municipality
to avoid incurrence of unnecessary bank service charges for issuing checks with
insufficient funds/cash bank balance.

1.6	We recommended for the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to strictly observe 	the prescribed timeline in the submission of contract and supporting documents which is five (5) days from its execution/perfection. 	Continued refusal to adhere as required shall subject concerned officials to legal consequences provided by law.

2.8	We recommended for the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to fill up 	immediately the mandatory positions for Municipal Health Officer and 	Municipal Treasurer of the LGU pursuant to the provision of the Local 	Government Code of 1991 in order to have an effective and efficient 	execution of public service and to facilitate the implementation of the 	plans and programs of the LGU.

3.12	We recommended that management implement immediately the 	unimplemented projects under the 20% DF and henceforth, implement 	within the timeframe as scheduled in the agency Annual Investment 	Program all development projects, consistent with the general policy of 	the government and mandated responsibility of LGU officials as 	embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG Memorandum Circular 2017-1, in 	order to attain desirable socio-economic development and 	environmental management outcomes.

4.8	We recommended for the LCE to instruct concerned officials to provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the Municipality’s total annual  	appropriation as required in DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Section 30 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act of CY 2018, we further recommend to fully utilize the funds. Likewise, the Municipality should prepare and submit the Accomplishment Report showing among others, the amount utilized in the implementation of projects lined up in the GAD Plan.

5.12	To address the issues, we recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor as Head should convene the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to create the Solid Waste Management Plan and for its implementation;
If feasible, assign additional personnel to assist the designated Solid Waste Management Officer who also functions as the Municipal Agricultural Officer
Integrate in the Annual Investment Plan the construction of the Sanitary Landfill and classify as urgent.  The construction of the sanitary landfill is one among the identified projects that is chargeable to the 20% Development Fund.

6.8 	To institutionalize the DRRM Office for the attainment of the objectives  	of the fund, we recommended that:
1. 	The Local Chief Executive with the help of the Municipal Budget Officer create items for three (3) staff in compliance with RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to undertake the setting of direction, development, and planning on the implementation and coordination of disaster risk management program.
2.  	The LDRRMO make a Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) and submit the same to the auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
3.  	The LCE though the LDRRM Office to maximize the use of the funds for the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities for prevention and mitigation and promote disaster preparedness.
4.  The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries and effect the actual transfer of the remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund to a special trust fund.

7.15	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to take necessary  	actions to ensure the implementation of the projects subject of the fund  	transfers as mandated in the Joint Circular. If on the other hand, the  	LGU in unable to implement the projects, the funds received should  	immediately be returned to the donor agency so it can be channeled to  	other government agencies where it can be put in to use instead of being  	dormant in depository banks.

To address the issues and concerns, we recommended that:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) as the chairman and all other members of the Local School Board (LSB) to prepare the Annual SEF Budget following the procedure set forth in the Joint Circular and furnish the Local Accountant, Local Treasurer and Local Budget a copy of the approved budget in the prescribed format;
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) observe the policies and guidelines contained in the joint circular to ensure a strategic and efficient utilization of resources for priority programs and projects which would complement the budgeting priorities at the different levels of decision-making for the welfare of its constituents; and
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to ensure that all public schools within its jurisdiction should equally avail of the Special Education Fund (SEF) for the benefit of all students thereof.

9.4	We recommended the Municipal Treasurer properly observe the requirements  	under COA Circular No. 92-389 in order to maintain a sound internal  	control in the utilization of government funds. The perforation of these documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but should include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which shall provide a space for the number of the check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces should be properly filled up.

10.9	We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of  	COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.  	2010-139. Prudent use of the scarce financial resources should be  	observed in sending municipal officials and employees to conventions,  	seminars and similar activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Sec. 20 of NGAS in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of each year based on the Certified list of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of Municipal Treasurer; and

Municipal Treasurer to intensify tax collections by formulating strategies to improve collection of real property taxes.

Reconciliation of the result of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record and investigate discrepancies as required in Section 491 of GAAM Volume I; and

Properly updated and maintained Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards by the OIC-General Service Officer and Accountant/Bookkeeper, respectively, in order to monitor the acquired assets of the municipality in compliance with Section 43 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason:

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Unit to immediately submit the required documents including its supporting papers to the Audit Team. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts will be detected immediately and adjusted in the books of accounts.  This will also minimize the accumulation of unreconciled adjustments of prior years’ transactions;

Accounting Office to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers of all bank accounts maintained by the agency for better monitoring of the movements of cash; and

Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records every quarter or as often as needed pursuant to the above cited provisions to closely monitor the correctness of the balances.

observations.recommendation:

Cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU officials/employees. Cause the withholding of salaries or any money due the officers and employees with overdue unliquidated cash advances to compel its settlement;

Ensure that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor for review. File appropriate charges, if warranted, for continued failure of the accountable officer to produce or liquidate the cash advance immediately upon demand of authorized officers.
observations.reason: Most of the concerned local officials and employees failed to liquidate/refund their outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation:

require each department heads to insure all government properties such as motor vehicles, machineries, permanent buildings and properties stored therein against any insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund by the GSIS; and

exert more effort to obtain records/documents on the Municipal Hall for a complete database of information and facilitate registration thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the list of delinquent stallholders together with other pertinent documents, like contract of lease to support the recording of rental receivables; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the unpaid space rentals, once the supporting list is ready, to ensure accuracy and reliability of the Agency’s Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:

Post the Invitation to Bid and other Notices to PhilGEPS website and in at least three conspicuous places in the Municipality to promote transparency and competitiveness in all aspects of procurement; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursements and scrutinize the completeness of supporting documents of claims before releasing the same for payment and prior to the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts will be detected immediately and adjusted in the books of accounts.  This will also minimize the accumulation of unreconciled adjustments of prior years’ transactions;
Accounting Office to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers of all bank accounts maintained by the agency for better monitoring of the movements of cash; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records every quarter or as often as needed pursuant to the above cited provisions to closely monitor the correctness of the balances.

We recommended that management require the:
Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Sec. 20 of NGAS in setting up the RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT Income at the beginning of each year;
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount collectible for the current year; and
Intensify tax collections by formulating strategies to improve collection of real property taxes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit year-end financial statement for all funds within the due dates as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 02, 2010.

We therefore recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Unit to immediately submit the required documents and supporting documents.  Henceforth, observe the timelines of reportorial requirements of the Commission as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended Municipality’s compliance on the provisions contained under Section 5.1.5 and relevant provisions regarding the utilization of 30% QRF of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to discontinue payment of membership dues of league/federation of appointed officials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with DBM Budget Execution Guidelines No. 2004-01 regarding the grant of honoraria to national agency officials assigned at the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) instruct the members of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as stipulated in Section 1 of R.A. No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160, Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-A, March 14, 2000; and (b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Prioritize the conduct of the physical inventory as required by existing regulations and reiterated under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016 by following the guidelines under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units Volume 1;
Reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the accounting and property records, and prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
Demand from the outgoing officials the return of the properties issued to them and all other documents and accountabilities that may be deemed necessary for the attainment of the Municipality’s objectives and mandates.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO to procure its common-use supplies from the PS – DBM in compliance with Executive Order 17 and Section 53(5) of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  In case of non-availability of items in the PS-DBM, we recommend that procurement of these common use goods should be in bulk quantities to obtain the most advantageous price.

We reiterated our earlier recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders/contacts and supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance/perfection to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
require the insurance of all government properties such as motor vehicles, machineries, permanent buildings and properties stored therein against any insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund by the GSIS; and
exert effort to submit the required documents to the GSIS to ensure release of  insurance policy of Foton ambulance.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the monthly monitoring report of the project status and inform the audit team of the posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in the aforementioned circular for validation.

We recommended that Management perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.  We also recommended that the Management immediately remit to BIR the amount of ₱20,062.32 which pertained to tax withheld in CY 2016.
We recommended that the Municipality exert effort in reconciling the undetermined amount of Due to GSIS balance as of December 31, 2016 and cause the remittance thereof.

We recommended that the management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to start the
reconciliation of their respective records by (i) assigning person/s who will
be responsible for property records reconciliation; (ii) regularly monitor
the progress of the property reconciliation; and (iii) conduct detailed and
complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and
document the same using the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain property cards for each
class of PPE account; (ii) secure the storage area by enclosing all possible
entries of theft in the area; and (iii) coordinate with the Human Resource
and Management Office (HRMO) to determine the last known addresses of
those employees who failed to surrender the property issued to them so that
demand/notice could be personally served to them or mailed, whichever is
possible under the circumstances;
c. Require those municipal officials and employees who have lost the
properties issued to them to prepare and submit Request for Relief from
Accountability duly supported with the required documents enumerated
under Section 499 of GAAM, Volume I and an explanation or justification
for the submission thereof beyond the thirty-day prescription period; and
d. Require the Municipal Treasurer to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for
Equipment (ARE) whenever property is issued to the end-user which
should be regularly updated at least every 3 years or upon change of
property accountability, such as during transfer and return/surrender of
property.

We recommended that the management:
a. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to invite more bidders in
their procurement process by posting the invitation on the Philippine
Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS), the website of the
Municipality and in at least three conspicuous places located in their
locality and in some nearby areas, if possible. Likewise, continuously utilize
the PhilGEPS in the dissemination of notices for purposes of promoting
transparency and competitiveness in all stages of the Municipality’s
procurement processes; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to refrain from preparing Certificate of
Inspection and Certificate of Project Completion with erasures or
handwritten particularly the dates of inspection, to clear doubt on whether
the projects inspected were actually completed by the contractor within the
period specified in the contract agreement, otherwise, direct the Municipal
Accountant not to honor documents which bear signs of tampering or false
representation.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer,
Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Budget Officer to immediately transmit
the unsubmitted accounts covering the previous period March to July, 2016
and November to December 2016, the 4th quarter financial reports and the
quarterly SAAOB for 2016; and henceforth adhere strictly to the timeframes
on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submittal of financial reports
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that management (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting additional cash
advances to officers and employees with outstanding balances and require
them to liquidate the same at year-end pursuant to the foregoing provisions;
and (b) cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances made by the former
Municipal Mayor totaling ₱461,000.00.

We recommended that the management:
a. Continue to plan socio-economic and environmental development
programs, projects and activities which partake the nature of investments
or capital expenditures to further enhance the delivery of basic services to
the local residents and implement the same promptly; and
b. Instruct the Local Finance Committee to monitor and ensure that no less
than 20% of the annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) share are
appropriated to the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the management (a) gather GAD information such as
gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning,
budgeting, programming and policy formulation for the setting up and
maintenance of GAD database; (b) submit budget realignment for the
augmentation made regarding PPAs dealing on malaria and those meant to
resolve issues on low visibility and lack of involvement of women sector in
community building and community affairs; (c) evaluate the GAD plan,
budget, and accomplishments to measure whether the activities undertaken by
the LGU addresses the real gender issues and concerns of men and women
within the locality and implement the same in an efficient and effective
manner; and (d) conduct a gender analysis of PPAs through the
administration of the HGDG test for effective budgeting.

We recommended that the management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Budget, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that at least 5% of the total estimated revenues from
regular sources are appropriated to the LDRRMF of the Municipality;
b. Make representation from Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the authority to
open and maintain a separate bank with an Authorized Government
Depository Bank (AGDB) to secure the Special Trust Fund from co-
mingling with the regular Trust Fund; and
c. Require the contractor concerned to undertake immediate repair on the
damaged portion of the retaining wall constructed at Dinumagat-Antipolo
in order to preserve its use and purpose, particularly in times of calamities,
such as typhoons, flood and heavy monsoon rain (habagat).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the various
appointed local officials to discontinue the payment of membership dues
including the charging of meals and snacks incurred during monthly
meetings or any assistance given in favor of leagues/associations; otherwise,
succeeding payments will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Human Resource Management Officer consider the necessity of
hiring additional traffic personnel to monitor and locate those tricycle
units operating without the required permit and license to prevent
undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated proliferation of tricycles
in remote places and along the municipal highways, which may pose
hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and
properties;
b. SB Members consider the inclusion of provisions in the local ordinance
regarding the issuance of identification cards to tricycle drivers and a
stricter enforcement of the wearing of prescribed uniform/dress code for
tricycle drivers to promote quality service favoring both tourists and local
residents; and
c. The Bids and Award Committee (BAC) Members to (i) undertake a
careful study and survey of the CCTV units to be procured to ensure that
the required specifications, quantity and the specific location where the
units will be installed are properly identified and estimated at the most
reasonable cost advantageous to the government; (ii) adhere to the laws,
rules and regulations provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of R.A. No. 9184; and (iii) consider the efficacy of the
equipment to be acquired in terms of the goal of the LGU towards the
promotion of public safety and order.

We recommended that the management:
a. Require the BuB Focal Person and Local Poverty Reduction Action Team
(LPRAT) to secure copies of the Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) from
concerned National Government Agencies (NGAs) to determine the
specific responsibilities of the LGU under the agreement; and support the
MOA with the prepared project proposals to guarantee the faithful
adherence by the LGU to the guidelines and procedures embodied therein
in the implementation of projects;
b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all
procurements are done through competitive bidding unless all the
conditions set forth for other modes of procurement under Sections 48 to
53 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are satisfactorily met to promote
transparency, economy and efficiency in operations;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to make sure that all issued properties
are evidenced by Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for
monitoring and fixing of accountability over the issued property;
d. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully check the completeness of
supporting documents such MOA/ARE and the like for each transaction
before releasing the same for payment; and
e. Instruct the BuB Focal Person and LPRAT to (1) make regular follow-up
with the Regional Poverty Reduction and Action Team (RPRAT) on the
status of the change of specification of the computer unit for DAP and
OSY Group project; and (2) plan and monitor the status of
implementation of all BuB projects.

We recommended that the management incorporate in its annual
appropriation the amount necessary to cover the insurance premiums of
their insurable properties to ensure that these are all adequately covered by
insurance with the GSIS to obtain maximum protection of the interest of the
government and contribute to revenue collection efforts of the National
Government as well.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support further the solid waste management
program of the government by continuously educating their respective
constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials
from compostable wastes;
b. Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board Member to monitor
the implementation of “no segregation, no collection policy” within the
Municipality;
c. Coordinate with the Zambales Electric Cooperative (Zameco II) Officials
for the necessary development/replacement of electrical materials to
improve power supply in the area;
d. Send personnel assigned in waste management in trainings, seminars and
let them observe best practices in other local government units in terms of
solid waste management plan;
e. Hire additional workforce for the maintenance and operation of the
Municipal MRCF and provide the necessary supplies and tools for their
safety and protection;
f. Coordinate continuously with the Department of Environment and
Natural Resources (DENR) in locating and acquiring a suitable lot for the
construction of sanitary landfill and secure Environmental Compliance
Certificate (ECC) before operation; and
g. Seek financial assistance from the national government for funding the
construction of Sanitary Landfill, and the improvement of Material
Recovery and Composting Facility.

We recommended that the management require the accountable officers to
settle their outstanding audit suspensions and/or disallowances through
compliance with the requirements, or payment/restitution thereof or by any
of the modes of extinguishment of obligation provided by law.

      Executive Order designating another Municipal Officer as a Chairperson

              and

              Acknowledgement Receipt.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor  require

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor  require

      dated December 19,  2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

           ➢ Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments  of  all
             valid reconciling items after:

               P154,683.72 between the ledger and the schedule;

           ➢ Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holders  of  checks

             advance/s  in  case  previous  cash  advance/s  have  not  been
             liquidated.

      Treasurer to:

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
      Accountant  to  prepare  the  necessary  adjusting  entries  for   the

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

             facilitate the titling of the untitled parcels of land  in  the

             government; and



           ➢ monitor the accomplishment of the OIC-  Municipal  Assessor  to
             ensure that subject lots are titled within the  targeted  time-
             frame and that funds for the purpose is  continuously  provided
             for all the untitled lots.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
      the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly
      to the concerned provincial and barangay governments their shares from
      real property tax and special education tax.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  the   Municipal
      Treasurer  see  to  it  that  all  the  insurable  properties  of  the

      No. 656.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor  require
      the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact and daily  or  on  the  next
      banking day all collections, in compliance  with  Section  32  of  the
      MNGAS, Volume I, to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.

      the Municipal Treasurer to:

           • require all the Collectors to remit their  collections  on  the
             day these are collected in compliance with Section 29  of  the
             MNGAS, Volume I, to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds; and


           • implement  rotation  of  collectors,  in  case  the  delays  in
             remittances are still incurred.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
      Treasurer  to  ensure  that  RCDs  are  properly  accomplished  before
      forwarding these to the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste
Management  Board,  expedite  the  revision  of  its  10-year  Solid   Waste
Management Plan and resubmit the same to the EMB for approval  by  the  NSWM
Commission.


          We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO  to
      provide technical assistance to barangays without  MRF  to  facilitate
      the establishment of the facility.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
      Budget Officer to allocate  one  percent  (1%)  of  its  IRA  for  the
      strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit a
timely and complete Bank Reconciliation Statement as required in Section 74
of PD 1445 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2,
1996 and to draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items
that require adjustment and correction in the GL as mandated in Sections
3.3 of the same COA Circular.

We recommend that the LCE direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to
deposit all collections intact with the depository bank daily or not later than
the next banking day as required in Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 8 and
28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 to safeguard funds from possible loss or
misappropriation.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant strictly observe the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 12-Inventories and
PPSAS 17-PPE insofar as the purchase of office supplies as well as drugs and
medicine and IT equipment is concerned to ensure proper accounting and
promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials
as well as the accuracy and reliability of inventory, PPE and expense
accounts as reflected in the financial statements.

We recommend that management’s key officials submit the
LDRRMFIP at the beginning of the year and the Report of Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after end of each month and
utilized the fund for eligible expenditures. Henceforth, comply strictly with
the pertinent provisions on the reporting and utilization guidelines of the
LDRRMF set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Accountant to submit the check disbursement accounts
together with the supporting documents and liquidation reports for the
period January to December, 2016 for proper evaluation on the utilization of
the Special Education Fund.

We recommend that the LCE advise the BAC to minimize the
practice of procuring goods and services through “shopping” and attach
BAC Resolutions to every procurement of goods and services. We likewise
recommend that Individual PPMPs of every department should be prepared
and submitted to be able to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan as guide
in the procurement of goods and services. Moreover, require the Municipal
Accountant to comply with the documentary requirements before payment
of procurement of goods and services is made.

We recommend that Management properly identify and fully
implement all eligible projects to be financed by the 20% development fund
within the budget year to achieve optimum desirable socio-economic and
environmental outcomes for its constituents consistent with the guidelines set
forth in the Joint DILG-DBM Circular.

We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of DSWD
Department Order No. 22, as amended by Administrative Order No. 5,
Series of 2008, and Section 4(2 and 6) of P. D. 1445 on the proper
documentation and evaluation of claims for financial assistance under the
AICs Program to ascertain its necessity & propriety.

We recommend that the Municipality create a Local Solid
Waste Management Board that will formulate a Solid Waste Management
Plan and work out for its approval to provide an efficient and effective system
of solid waste collection and disposal within its area of jurisdiction. Moreover,
the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities in barangays as well as
common waste treatment and disposal facilities be strictly implemented to
safeguard public health and the environment.

We recommend that management strictly observe the timely
submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section
7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section
39(1) and (3) of PD 1445.
